name: Bill Finegan
description: American bandleader, pianist, arranger, and composer (1917-2008)
abstract: William James Finegan (April 3, 1917 – June 4, 2008) was an American jazz bandleader, pianist, arranger, and composer. He was an arranger in the Glenn Miller Orchestra in the late 1930s and early 1940s.

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: So we got to be good friends. And it’s funny. Right up to the very end, Bill would call me… just two or three years ago... So that was our… It was a friendship and…
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Shiner
about_quotes.evidence: I says to Finegan, 'How come you picked me to play oboe in the band?' ... 'And the guys in the band wanted you.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Shiner interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 11, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Andre
about_quotes.evidence: Well you were in the Sauter-Finegan band... I auditioned... for the Sauter-Finegan band... I got the job.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: James Chirillo
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Finegan told me this one... Bill said, he had a little like downstairs room... Bill wrote three arrangements on that.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Chirillo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: John LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: by studying the Glenn Miller scores, like Bill Finegan’s scores and stuff like that, I learned a lot.

about_quotes.speaker: DeFranco Buddy
about_quotes.evidence: I got a hold of Bill Finegan again and asked him to write a couple new arrangements
about_quotes.doc_title: DeFranco Buddy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
